assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 87-112 detail all 5 criteria individually marked PASS: (1) single license filter, (2) comma-separated filter, (3) 400 validation for invalid SPDX, (4) pagination integration, (5) unchanged response shape (PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>). Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each contain 'Verdict: PASS' with detailed code-level reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 3 files specified in the task (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files'. Lines 27-35 provide detailed evidence: 'Task Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs -- both present in PR diff' and 'Task Files to Create: tests/api/package.rs -- present in PR diff as a new file', with 'Out-of-scope files: none' and 'Unimplemented files: none'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. The report contains no mention of creating sub-tasks. The overall verdict is PASS with no issues requiring attention (line 19).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all markdown report files. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report content describes only reading and analyzing the diff (e.g., 'Scanned all added lines', 'Examined 4 test functions', per-criterion code-level verification), with no indication of code modifications. All detailed findings sections describe verification activities, not code changes.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'All checks pass. The PR cleanly implements the license filter feature as specified in TC-9101 with no issues requiring attention.' (report.md lines 17-19). There is no merge command, no auto-merge action, and no indication that the PR was merged. The report presents findings (detailed verdicts, evidence, and related review comments sections) for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No sensitive patterns detected in added lines; no hardcoded passwords, API keys, private keys, or credentials found across all 3 files'. Lines 63-69 detail the security scan: 'Scanned all added lines (lines with + prefix) in the PR diff', 'Checked for: hardcoded passwords/secrets, API keys/tokens, private keys/certificates, .env files, cloud provider credentials, database credentials', 'No matches found. The diff contains only Rust source code with type definitions, validation logic, query builder modifications, and test assertions.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (tests/api/package.rs with 4 new test functions); no existing tests modified or deleted'. Lines 162-170 provide detailed evidence: 'The only test file in the PR is tests/api/package.rs, which is a newly created file (not present on the base branch). New test files are inherently additive.' with 'New file: tests/api/package.rs -- 80 lines, 4 test functions, all new', 'Modified test files: none', 'Deleted test files: none', 'Classification: ADDITIVE (new tests only, no coverage reduction)'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md summary table (lines 5-15) contains all expected verdicts: Review Feedback (N/A), Root-Cause Investigation (N/A), Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS), Sensitive Patterns (PASS), CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Test Quality (PASS), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE), Verification Commands (N/A). The detailed findings section organizes them by domain: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 25-57) contains Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability; 'Security' (lines 59-71) contains Sensitive Pattern Scan; 'Correctness' (lines 73-119) contains CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands; 'Style/Conventions' (lines 121-172) contains Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification. Test Quality in the summary (line 13) combines 'no repetitive tests detected', doc comments assessment, and 'Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 26-57): file-by-file scope comparison listing 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs' as modified and 'tests/api/package.rs' as created, with line counts (~106 additions, ~2 deletions, ~108 total). Security (lines 63-69): line-level scanning details — 'Scanned all added lines (lines with + prefix)', checked for 6 specific pattern categories, notes that 'Test data uses SPDX license identifiers (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-only) and package names (pkg-a, pkg-b, etc.) which are non-sensitive.' Correctness (lines 87-112): per-criterion code-level verification with specific code references (PackageListParams, validate_license_param, Condition::any(), is_in filter, AppError::BadRequest) and test function names. Style/Conventions (lines 130-170): individual test function analysis with quoted doc comments, parameterization assessment per Meszaros heuristic, and per-file classification.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13, Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. Lines 156-159: 'Eval Quality -- N/A', 'Details: No eval result reviews found on this PR. No eval quality assessment applicable.' The Test Quality summary (line 13) is PASS, combining repetitive test detection and test documentation results without being affected by the N/A Eval Quality.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md lines 156-159). No sub-tasks of any kind are mentioned as created in the report. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. The report concludes with 'no issues requiring attention' (line 19), confirming no sub-tasks were generated.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These two verdicts appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) but do NOT appear under any of the four domain subsections (Intent Alignment lines 26-57, Security lines 59-71, Correctness lines 73-119, Style/Conventions lines 121-172). This confirms they are determined independently by the orchestrator, separate from the domain sub-agent analysis.
